response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer (~13+ years) with strong fundamentals in testing, architecture, and multi-language development, but the resume shows minimal recent C++ work — only a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015. The role demands hands-on C++/STL expertise, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 security standards, none of which are evident in recent experience. Strengths in DLang, Node.js, and distributed systems are transferable but do not substitute for the required C++ depth in industrial automation.
strengths: 13+ years of software engineering with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby
Strong testing/TDD discipline and mentoring background (workshops, dojos, Selenium course)
Distributed systems and event-driven architecture exposure via GISCollective and microservices work
concerns: C++ experience is over a decade old and limited to ~6 months at National Instruments
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No evidence of MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or industrial automation protocol experience

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial automation and IEC 62443 background
Senior-level engineering breadth and architecture experience
